features.name: Optimize dashboard alarm rendering
features.description: Limit dashboard alarm rows and run the overlay flash timer only while red alarms exist.
evidence: Dashboard flash timer now starts only when a red alarm exists and is stopped and reset when no red alarm remains or the component unmounts.
Initial and WebSocket alarm lists are capped at 100 rows in both the dashboard display state and Pinia alarm store.
.\init.cmd passed on 2026-07-13.
npm.cmd --prefix frontend run build passed on 2026-07-13 with 1668 modules transformed.
blockers: Chrome Performance before/after profiling and the 10-minute continuous-alarm browser acceptance test still require an interactive live stream session.

features.name: Initialize repository and publish markdown docs
features.description: Initialize the local project as a Git repository and push the markdown project documents to GitHub.
evidence: Created init.sh as the standard smoke-test entry point.
Added init.ps1 and init.cmd so Windows PowerShell can run the same smoke test through .\init.cmd without depending on WSL or direct .sh execution.
Created feature_list.json as the feature status source of truth.
PowerShell-equivalent smoke test passed because this Windows host only exposes the WSL bash shim and has no installed WSL distribution.
Merged remote main history without file conflicts.
Pushed local markdown documentation and project metadata to origin/main.

features.name: Create project README
features.description: Create a repository README that summarizes the project purpose, architecture, feature modules, document structure, and validation entry point.
evidence: Created README.md based on PRD.md and 系统设计说明书.md.
Smoke test passed after README creation.
README update was pushed to origin/main.

features.name: Standardize OpenSpec module workflow
features.description: Make OpenSpec the required, repository-local workflow for every new module and cross-module change.
evidence: Pinned @fission-ai/openspec 1.4.1 in the root tooling package and added repository-local npm commands for list, validate, status, new change, and archive.

Restored OpenSpec project context and capability index, documented legacy proposals as read-only references, and retained intrusion-detect-enhance as the active change for the complete seat-reservation intrusion work.
Expanded all six baseline capability purposes so npm run spec:validate passed with 7 items and 0 failures in strict mode.

Updated AGENTS.md, README.md, init.cmd, init.ps1, and init.sh so strict OpenSpec validation is a required startup gate and PowerShell uses the local npm-installed CLI instead of the policy-blocked global openspec.ps1.
On 2026-07-13, npm run spec:list showed intrusion-detect-enhance in progress and npm run spec:status reported all 4 required artifacts complete.
blockers: This Windows host exposes the WSL bash shim but has no usable Git Bash path, so init.sh was updated by parity with init.ps1 but not executed locally; init.cmd passed.

features.name: Stabilize Jenkins pipeline configuration
features.description: Replace the fragile credential/deploy-heavy Jenkins pipeline with a cross-platform CI pipeline that Jenkins can schedule from SCM reliably.
evidence: Simplified Jenkinsfile to standard Pipeline from SCM stages: Checkout, Smoke Test, Frontend Build, Backend Syntax Check, and Docker Image.
Removed hard requirements on optional Jenkins plugins, Docker Hub credentials, DingTalk credentials, placeholder model download URLs, and production deploy paths from the default CI path.
Jenkinsfile now supports both Unix and Windows agents through isUnix(), sh, and bat branches.
Backend syntax check uses PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=.pycache-ci so stale or locked __pycache__ directories do not break Jenkins agents.
.\init.cmd passed on this Windows host.
Frontend production build passed with npm.cmd run build.
Backend compileall passed with PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=.pycache-ci.
blockers: Local Docker validation could not complete because this Windows user cannot read C:\Users\wangyihan\.docker\config.json and cannot connect to the Docker API pipe. Jenkins Docker stage still requires a Jenkins agent with Docker installed and permission to access the daemon.

Jenkins UI error 'Failed to schedule build' can also be caused by job-level settings outside the repository, such as a disabled job, missing build permission, zero executors, offline agents, quiet-down mode, or a Pipeline SCM branch/Jenkinsfile path mismatch.

features.name: Task C3 fire and smoke detection plugin
features.description: Implement the YOLO-backed fire/smoke detector plugin with 30-frame confidence-window debouncing and AlarmEvent(type=fire_smoke) output.
required_validation: python -m pytest tests/test_intrusion.py tests/test_fight.py tests/test_fight_integration.py tests/test_face.py tests/test_alarm_center.py tests/test_fire_smoke.py
evidence: Implemented backend/app/detectors/fire_smoke.py with FireSmokeDetector.feed() preserving FIRE_WINDOW/FIRE_CONF sliding-window logic.

Added FireSmokePlugin(Detector) with setup() YOLO weight loading, fire/smoke class filtering, max-confidence extraction, and AlarmEvent(type=fire_smoke) output including camera_id, ts, snapshot, confidence, and extra metadata.
Registered FireSmokePlugin in backend/run.py so it is managed by InferenceEngine without creating detector-local threads or loops.
Added backend/tests/test_fire_smoke.py covering full-window debounce, single-frame flash rejection, fire/smoke class acceptance, non-fire/smoke filtering, and empty-weight rejection.
pytest passed: tests/test_fire_smoke.py -> 6 passed.
Backend related pytest passed: 38 passed for intrusion, fight, fight integration, face, alarm center, and fire smoke tests.
Backend smoke test passed after restoring merge-damaged foundation pieces.
PowerShell-equivalent init smoke passed with 15 markdown docs.
Added Swagger docs for fire/smoke integration endpoints: cameras, regions, alarms, snapshots, and WebSocket stream/alarm paths. Verified /apispec_1.json contains FireSmoke on 11 required operations.

Integrated the repository-local fire-smoke-detect-yolov4-master/yolov5 legacy YOLOv5 checkpoint through a backend compatibility adapter after confirming the YOLOv4 weight placeholder is empty and the usable model is yolov5/best.pt with fire/smoke classes.
Copied yolov5/best.pt locally to backend/model_weights/fire_smoke.pt; the file is non-empty and gitignored as a model artifact.
Real legacy checkpoint load passed through FireSmokePlugin fallback: Ultralytics YOLOv8 rejected the old YOLOv5 pickle, then LegacyYolov5FireSmokeModel loaded it with names ['fire', 'smoke'].

Demo image validation passed on fire-smoke-detect-yolov4-master/result/result_demo.jpg: model detected fire with confidence about 0.662 and smoke with confidence about 0.311; 30-frame debouncing produced AlarmEvent(type=fire_smoke).

Validation passed after integration: py_compile for config/fire_smoke/legacy_yolov5/test_fire_smoke, test_fire_smoke.py -> 7 passed, related backend regression -> 44 passed, backend smoke -> ALL SMOKE TESTS PASSED, real DB verify -> TASK_E_REAL_DB_VERIFY_OK.
Added backend service facade app.services.fire_smoke.detect_fire_smoke_image() so direct backend startup can call the grafted legacy YOLOv5 fire/smoke model without relying on standalone scripts.

Added POST /api/alarms/fire-smoke/detect, accepting multipart image upload or JSON/form image_path, with frames support for the 30-frame debounce window and optional raise_alarm=true persistence/broadcast.

Real backend service validation passed on test_photos/fire_test.jpg: legacy YOLOv5 fallback loaded backend/model_weights/fire_smoke.pt, detected fire/smoke, and emitted one fire_smoke event after 30 frames.

Validation after backend-callable integration passed: py_compile for fire_smoke detector/service/API/tests, focused pytest 18 passed, related backend regression 45 passed, backend smoke test ALL SMOKE TESTS PASSED, and init.cmd smoke passed.

Changed the default fire/smoke model loader to legacy YOLOv5 so local scripts and backend startup no longer stall while importing ultralytics/torch before falling back. Validation passed: raw image script detected fire/smoke on test_photos/fire_test.jpg, alarm image script emitted one fire_smoke event after 30 frames, focused pytest 19 passed, and init.cmd smoke passed.
Current workspace validation on 2026-07-12 passed through the repository .venv: test_fire_smoke_image.py detected fire 0.757, fire 0.557, and smoke 0.256 on test_photos/fire_test.jpg.

Current workspace validation on 2026-07-12 passed through the repository .venv: test_fire_smoke_alarm_image.py repeated the same test image for 30 frames and emitted one AlarmEvent(type=fire_smoke, camera_id=5, level=1, confidence about 0.757).
Current workspace validation on 2026-07-12 passed: backend tests/test_fire_smoke.py -> 8 passed, and .\init.cmd smoke passed.

features.name: Task E alarm center and DingTalk close loop
features.description: Implement the unified alarm event contract, alarm persistence/snapshot/dedup service, alarm WebSocket push, DingTalk primary/escalation workflow, and alarm query/confirm APIs.
required_validation: python -m pytest tests/test_intrusion.py tests/test_fight.py tests/test_fight_integration.py tests/test_face.py tests/test_alarm_center.py
evidence: Updated AlarmEvent with type, region_id, camera_id, ts, level, snapshot_url, face_match, extra, while preserving legacy confidence/snapshot compatibility.

Implemented AlarmService raise_alarm() with 30s region/type dedup, snapshot persistence, intrusion/occupy face matching fallback, alarm_event persistence, level=0 private-only handling, WebSocket broadcast, and DingTalk notification trigger.
Implemented /ws/alarms broadcast registration and /api/alarms query, confirm, and snapshot access endpoints.
Implemented DingTalkNotifier notify/confirm/escalate with notification_log primary/escalated records, status transitions, timer cancellation, and no-webhook local-safe behavior.
Extended ORM models for camera_id, fight alarm type, extra JSON, Guard, notification_log guard FK, and indexes.
Added backend/tests/test_alarm_center.py covering persistence/snapshot/notify, dedup, level=0 private-only behavior, DingTalk confirm/escalation logs, and alarm HTTP API.
pytest passed: 27 passed for intrusion, fight, fight integration, face, and alarm center tests.

Real MySQL verification passed against DATABASE_URI from .env: connected to study_room, seeded minimal camera/region/guards, wrote confirmed fight, escalated intrusion, private fatigue alarms, and verified DingTalk notification logs without exposing secrets.
Added backend/scripts/verify_task_e_real_db.py as the repeatable real-database verification entry point.
backend app config now loads .env automatically and supports both KEY= and PowerShell $env:KEY= syntax.
backend/requirements.txt includes PyMySQL for mysql+pymysql DATABASE_URI values.
backend/tests/smoke_test.py passed after restoring StreamScheduler ring_buffer maxlen to 5.
PowerShell-equivalent init smoke passed with 16 markdown docs.
Real DingTalk text webhook connectivity was verified with errcode=0 after DINGTALK_WEBHOOK was added to .env.
Added DingTalk signed webhook support via DINGTALK_SECRET and DINGTALK_LEADER_SECRET, plus public ActionCard confirm/snapshot URLs via PUBLIC_BASE_URL.
Added GET /api/alarms/{id}/confirm for DingTalk ActionCard button confirmation while preserving the existing POST confirm API.
Regression after DingTalk real-integration prep passed: 28 task-E-related tests, backend smoke test with ALL SMOKE TESTS PASSED, and real MySQL verify script.
Real DingTalk ActionCard smoke send through DingTalkNotifier returned errcode=0; click-confirm still awaits PUBLIC_BASE_URL.
After pulling origin/main on 2026-07-09, removed duplicate ws.register_ws_routes(sock) registration in backend/app/__init__.py and verified python tests/smoke_test.py passed again.

Post-merge validation repaired duplicate WebSocket registration, duplicate Guard ORM definition, missing AlarmService helper methods, and FaceMatcher immediate AlarmEvent compatibility; validation passed with bash ./init.sh, 39 pytest cases including fire_smoke, smoke test, and real MySQL verify.

Real DingTalk close-loop send test passed on 2026-07-09: primary ActionCard returned errcode=0, a 5-second unconfirmed timer escalated the alarm to status=escalated/level=2, and the escalated ActionCard also returned errcode=0.
The real escalation test wrote notification_log rows for guard role primary and guard role leader, confirming DB-level guard selection for primary/escalated stages.

Fixed local DingTalk confirm-button testing by changing .env PUBLIC_BASE_URL from an RTMP stream URL to http://127.0.0.1:5000; a new ActionCard for alarm 16 was sent successfully with an HTTP confirm URL.

Enhanced DingTalk alarm cards to show actor, behavior, alarm type, handler, location, camera, snapshot, and score context; added a separate text @ message to mention the selected guard by guard.dingtalk_id.
Real DingTalk actor/handler smoke passed for alarm 17: ActionCard returned errcode=0 with HTTP confirm URL and the follow-up text message with at payload also returned errcode=0.
Enhanced the DingTalk ActionCard backend payload to include the persisted alarm message, public snapshot markdown image, and clip playback URL when available.
Enhanced the DingTalk GET confirm page so clicking Confirm now confirms the alarm and returns a detail page with message, status, snapshot image, clip video/link, and detector extra context.

Added Swagger documentation for task-E REST integration points: alarm listing, JSON confirm, DingTalk ActionCard HTML confirm, and snapshot access; /apispec_1.json exposes the AlarmEvent and confirm response schemas.

Added a Task E local integration endpoint POST /api/alarms/test-capture that pulls one frame from an RTMP/RTSP/video source or camera.stream_url, saves the snapshot through AlarmService, persists alarm_event, broadcasts WebSocket payloads, and triggers DingTalk for level>=1 alarms.

Task E self-capture validation passed: py_compile for alarms.py/stream_capture.py/test_alarm_center.py, focused alarm-center pytest with 10 passed, related backend regression pytest with 43 passed, and backend smoke with ALL SMOKE TESTS PASSED.

Real RTMP stream integration: backend successfully connected to cloud RTMP server at 49.233.71.82:9090, decoder statistics showed ok=129 frames with dropped=0. Fixed scheduler.py to remove live=1 from URL and add rtmp_live;live as FFmpeg option. Fixed camera_id=0 boundary checks in alarms.py. Enhanced stream_capture.py to prioritize scheduler ring buffer over opening a second RTMP connection.

Made stream startup configurable with STREAM_CAMERA_ID/STREAM_NAME/STREAM_URL/STREAM_LOCAL_CAMERA. Default startup camera is camera_id=5, and StreamScheduler.add_camera(camera_id=5) now loads camera.stream_url from the database when no explicit stream name or URL is provided.

Added scheduler tests for database stream_url resolution, legacy RTMP stream-name fallback, and local camera integer handling. Validation passed: py_compile via WSL, tests/test_task_g.py -> 6 passed, tests/test_alarm_center.py tests/test_task_g.py -> 16 passed, and backend smoke printed ALL SMOKE TESTS PASSED.

Real camera verification script was fixed to keep the requested --camera-id when deriving region_id. A run with --camera-id 5 resolved camera_id=5, region_id=5, source=rtmp://49.233.71.82:9090/live/test, then timed out waiting for a frame after the RTMP source did not produce readable frames.

Added --stream-url to verify_task_e_g_real_camera.py so a real RTMP source can be tested with any existing camera/region row. Retesting with camera_id=1 and stream_url=rtmp://49.233.71.82:9090/live/test still failed at RTMP handshake, confirming the current blocker is not camera_id binding.

Added GET /api/cameras/{camera_id}/stream-status to inspect whether StreamScheduler is running, camera is registered/online, and decoded JPEG/pre-buffer frames are available. Updated stream_capture to wait the full timeout for scheduler frames before opening a second RTMP connection. Validation passed: py_compile, tests/test_task_g.py tests/test_alarm_center.py -> 17 passed, and backend smoke printed ALL SMOKE TESTS PASSED.

Clean restart after stopping duplicate Python listeners on port 5000 fixed the confusing stale-process state. python backend/run.py connected to camera_id=5 at rtmp://49.233.71.82:9090/live/test with decoder stats ok frames and 0 dropped.

Real Task E/G verification succeeded through the running backend scheduler: /api/cameras/5/stream-status returned online=true and has_frame=true, POST /api/alarms/test-capture created alarm_id=48 with snapshot_url, async recording filled clip_url=/api/alarms/clips/alarm_48_1783691900602.mp4, snapshot returned 200 with 17558 bytes, clip returned 200 with 227218 bytes, and daily report for 2026-07-10 returned 200.
Added alarm log system: each alarm trigger records detailed information to backend/logs/alarm_YYYY-MM-DD.log including id, type, level, region, camera, face_match, message, snapshot_url, and extra.
Added StorageManager with tiered cleanup strategy: warning threshold 80%, critical threshold 90%, automatic cleanup of expired snapshots, clips, and logs.
Optimized DingTalk notification: snapshot converted to Base64 and embedded directly in message to solve public IP access issues.
Optimized image compression: JPEG quality 60%, max resolution 1280x720.
Confirm page uses relative paths for resources, supporting access from any address.
Added GET /api/alarms/storage-status endpoint to query storage status.
Real alarm test passed: alarm_id=52 created with snapshot (4364 bytes), clip recording (10 frames), DingTalk message sent with Base64 image (5820 bytes), status=200.
Fixed face_spoof alarm routing: added face_spoof to alarm_type enum in entities.py and init.sql, fixed missing camera_id in face.py, added face_spoof handler in alarm.py, database enum updated.
Fixed sliding window voting in face.py: removed premature return that skipped de-duplication logic for stranger/face_spoof alarms.
Validation passed: all alarm types (intrusion, fire_smoke, occupy, fatigue, fight, face_recognition, face_spoof) can now trigger DingTalk notifications correctly.

Post-review repair on 2026-07-11 fixed a committed FaceDetector indentation error while preserving normal face_recognition as WebSocket-only and face_spoof as an AlarmEvent path; tests/test_face.py tests/test_alarm_center.py passed with 26 cases.

DingTalk ActionCard extra/context format was changed from field-style score output to a spoken summary such as '请 Primary Guard 处理：系统在 Seat A1 的 Reading Room Camera 发现 Li Ming 存在“pushed another student”的情况...'.

2026-07-12 alarm capture review: stream_capture now configures the same low-latency RTMP/FFmpeg OpenCV options used by StreamScheduler before one-shot capture, and it no longer falls back to frames from other registered cameras when the requested camera_id is not in the scheduler.

Validation on 2026-07-12: .\init.cmd passed; WSL py_compile passed for backend/app/services/stream_capture.py and backend/tests/test_alarm_center.py. Full pytest could not complete in the available local environments because WSL lacks pytest and Anaconda pytest lacks flask_cors plus has a Temp pytest directory permission error.

2026-07-12 DingTalk backend verification: POST /api/alarms/test-capture created alarm 73, alarm_event moved to escalated level=3, notification_log contains primary guard_id=3 and escalated guard_id=5 rows, and a real UTF-8 DingTalk webhook text request with alarm keywords returned errcode=0.

2026-07-12 DingTalk confirm page simplified: GET /api/alarms/{id}/confirm still confirms through backend and broadcasts WebSocket updates, but no longer renders alarm details, snapshot URLs, or clip URLs. Validation passed with py_compile, init.cmd, and live confirm page check for alarm 73 showing no media links.
blockers: bash ./init.sh now passes after Ubuntu WSL was installed; WSL still prints a localhost proxy warning in NAT mode.
Full DingTalk ActionCard click-confirm verification still needs PUBLIC_BASE_URL pointing at a public tunnel to the backend.
DINGTALK_LEADER_WEBHOOK is not configured, so escalation currently reuses the primary group robot; true separate leader-group delivery needs a leader webhook.
The @ mention payload is now sent, but real human notification still depends on guard.dingtalk_id being a DingTalk user ID or mobile accepted by the robot.

features.name: Task G violation snapshot playback and AI daily report
features.description: Enhance the alarm center with pre/post-event video clip evidence, HTTP playback, stream diagnostics, and daily monitoring report generation.
required_validation: Real RTMP scheduler capture, snapshot, clip playback, and daily report verification
evidence: AlarmEvent includes clip_url, and AlarmService starts asynchronous clip recording after snapshot persistence so the alarm path returns before MP4 generation completes.
StreamScheduler maintains pre-event JPEG frames, exposes camera status diagnostics, and can load camera.stream_url by STREAM_CAMERA_ID or explicit STREAM_URL/STREAM_LOCAL_CAMERA config.
ClipRecorder generates MP4 files under CLIP_DIR, updates alarm_event.clip_url, and broadcasts a WebSocket clip_url update when the clip becomes available.
GET /api/alarms/clips/{filename} serves saved MP4 evidence with HTTP Range support for playback seeking.

DailyReportService in backend/app/services/daily_report.py generates JSON and Markdown monitoring reports, saves report_<date>.json and report_<date>.md artifacts, and exposes backend/scripts/daily_report.py for scheduled daily generation.
ClipRecorder has a CLIP_MAX_DAYS cleanup strategy that removes expired video artifacts before new recording jobs.

Task G formal regression coverage now lives in backend/tests/test_alarm_center.py on the current taskE branch, covering confirm-page snapshot/clip/detail rendering, clip HTTP Range playback, and daily-report JSON/Markdown artifact generation.

Backend-only regression passed after alarm-center refinements: py_compile for changed backend files, tests/test_alarm_center.py -> 14 passed, related backend pytest excluding the independent liveness assertion -> 61 passed, backend smoke printed ALL SMOKE TESTS PASSED, and init.cmd passed.

Real RTMP verification succeeded after clean backend restart: /api/cameras/5/stream-status returned online=true and has_frame=true, POST /api/alarms/test-capture created alarm_id=48 with a snapshot, async recording produced /api/alarms/clips/alarm_48_1783691900602.mp4, snapshot and clip endpoints returned HTTP 200, and /api/alarms/daily-report?date=2026-07-10 returned HTTP 200 including alarm 48.

2026-07-12 playback repair: AlarmService now starts clip recording before DingTalk notification, ClipRecorder uses the actual recording start time for post-event capture, captures an immediate latest frame, verifies encoded output, resizes mismatched frames, and pads very short clips to at least 1 second to avoid 0s playback.

2026-07-12 real server playback test: camera 5 stream-status returned online=true, has_frame=true, pre_buffer_len=75; POST /api/alarms/test-capture created alarm 72 with snapshot /api/alarms/snapshots/alarm_1783823167935_5_fight.jpg and clip /api/alarms/clips/alarm_72_1783823167935.mp4; snapshot HTTP returned 200 image/jpeg length 19349; clip file was 864818 bytes, OpenCV read 112 frames at 15 FPS for about 7.47s, HTTP full clip returned 200 video/mp4 and Range request returned 206 with Content-Range bytes 0-31/864818.
blockers: Task G backend capture/playback/report flow is complete; frontend playback UI is owned by Task F and existing staged frontend files were not changed in this backend verification pass.

Fight clips produced by the current alarm-center OpenCV fallback are video-only. If the team requires audio evidence, A/D should provide a muxed clip or audio stream interface; the alarm center can persist and expose the resulting clip_url.
If live capture fails again, first check for duplicate Python processes listening on port 5000 before changing RTMP code.

features.name: Implement Task B fatigue detection and study companion
features.description: Implement Dlib-landmark EAR/MAR fatigue detection, the FatiguePlugin, seat status persistence and hot reload, and configurable fatigue alarm level for DingTalk reminders.
required_validation: python -m py_compile backend/app/detectors/fatigue.py backend/app/api/seat_status.py backend/app/models/entities.py backend/app/config.py backend/app/services/alarm.py backend/app/detectors/face.py backend/app/__init__.py backend/app/stream/scheduler.py backend/run.py backend/tests/test_fatigue.py
python -m pytest tests/test_fatigue.py tests/test_alarm_center.py tests/test_face.py tests/test_intrusion.py tests/test_fight.py tests/test_fight_integration.py
evidence: Created branch codex/task-b-fatigue-companion from latest origin/main after stashing pre-existing local dirty work.
Fixed init.sh to validate root PRD.md after the PRD was moved out of openspec/specs.
Implemented FatigueDetector EAR/MAR state machine with sleepy/yawn outcomes and corrected inner-mouth 60-67 MAR indexing.
Implemented FatiguePlugin with Dlib landmark loading, active studying-seat reload, per-region state, hot update handling, and configurable AlarmEvent(type=fatigue) reminders.
Implemented POST /api/seat-status validation, seat-region requirement, application-level upsert, and fatigue plugin hot reload through the live StreamScheduler engine.
Registered FatiguePlugin in backend/run.py and exposed StreamScheduler.engine for API-triggered hot updates.

Fixed baseline blockers discovered by regression: duplicate Guard ORM model, missing AlarmService helper methods, FaceDetector mock fallback, duplicate WebSocket route registration, and local default DATABASE_URI fallback.
Added backend/tests/test_fatigue.py covering EAR duration, reset, yawn, level=0 fatigue events, resting disable, seat-status validation/upsert, hot reload, and multi-seat enable behavior.
py_compile passed for modified backend files and the new fatigue test.
pytest passed: 41 passed for fatigue, alarm center, face, intrusion, fight, and fight integration tests.
backend/tests/smoke_test.py passed.
PowerShell-equivalent init smoke passed with 15 markdown docs.
Docker dev runtime prepared: backend uses stable image ai-study-room-backend-env:dev, mounts ../backend and ../backend/model_weights, and runs gunicorn --reload for code reload without image rebuild.
Container runtime verified: dlib 19.24.9, cv2 4.9.0, shape_predictor_68_face_landmarks.dat present, demo seat region 5 set to studying, and POST /api/seat-status returned success.
RTMP pipeline debug verified with a temporary local test stream; after user clarification, Docker runtime defaults were corrected to the cloud RTMP server 49.233.71.82:9090.
Cloud RTMP runtime verified: backend pulled rtmp://49.233.71.82:9090/live/test successfully and persisted level=0 fatigue alarms with extra.kind=sleepy.
Fixed AlarmService camera_id persistence so camera_id=0 is stored as 0 instead of NULL; focused alarm/fatigue tests passed.
Pulled latest frontend files from origin/main and integrated /api/cameras so the dashboard can resolve the cloud RTMP camera stream into an HTTP-FLV playback URL; frontend production build passed.
Changed fatigue alerts to use configurable FATIGUE_ALERT_LEVEL, defaulting to 1 so studying-seat sleepy/yawn events enter the existing DingTalk notification path.
pytest passed: 15 passed for tests/test_fatigue.py and tests/test_alarm_center.py after adding coverage that level=1 fatigue alarms notify.
Docker backend was recreated without image rebuild; container config confirmed FATIGUE_ALERT_LEVEL=1 and DINGTALK_WEBHOOK unset.

Container smoke alarm id 48 persisted as type=fatigue level=1 status=notified with a primary notification_log record, proving the DingTalk notifier path is invoked; real external DingTalk send remains blocked until DINGTALK_WEBHOOK is configured.
blockers: bash ./init.sh still cannot run on this Windows host because bash.exe is the WSL shim and no WSL distribution is installed; PowerShell-equivalent smoke was used and passed.

Runtime Dlib fatigue inference requires backend/model_weights/shape_predictor_68_face_landmarks.dat or MODEL_DIR to point at that file; this workspace currently has the file, deployments must also provide it.
./init.sh cannot be executed directly from this PowerShell session due OS permission/WSL shim behavior; py_compile and Docker runtime checks were used for this Docker-focused pass.
Real DingTalk external send is not verified because DINGTALK_WEBHOOK is not set in the current Docker environment.

features.name: Verify Task B danger-zone intrusion end to end
features.description: Verify frontend danger-zone save API, backend hot reload, intrusion detection, alarm persistence, snapshot creation, and alarm query visibility.
required_validation: docker compose logs show intrusion active danger zones updated
docker compose exec backend PYTHONPATH=/app python scripts/verify_intrusion_e2e.py
python -m py_compile backend/scripts/verify_intrusion_e2e.py backend/app/detectors/intrusion.py backend/app/api/regions.py backend/run.py
evidence: Implemented database-backed /api/regions CRUD with validation for polygon >= 3 points, x_distance >= 0, y_stay_time >= 0, and type in danger_zone/seat.
Implemented IntrusionPlugin registration in backend/run.py and restored PersonDetector + per-region IntrusionDetector runtime loading from the region table.
POST /api/regions created danger_zone id=6 named E2E Intrusion Zone for camera_id=0 with polygon [[0,0],[640,0],[640,360],[0,360]].
GET /api/regions?camera_id=0 returned region id=6 with type=danger_zone and the saved polygon, matching the frontend draw/save data shape.
Backend logs showed [engine] registered detector intrusion and [intrusion] active danger zones: [6], proving hot reload consumed the saved region.
Container verification script scripts/verify_intrusion_e2e.py simulated a person box entering region 6 through IntrusionPlugin and persisted alarm id=103.
GET /api/alarms?status=notified returned alarm id=103 with type=intrusion, region_id=6, camera_id=0, status=notified, face_match=stranger, and person_box evidence.
Snapshot file /app/snapshots/alarm_6_intrusion_1783691102938.jpg exists for alarm id=103.
Focused backend tests passed: python -m pytest tests/test_intrusion.py tests/test_alarm_center.py -> 9 passed.
Frontend production build passed via npm.cmd --prefix frontend run build.
blockers: Live cloud RTMP rtmp://49.233.71.82:9090/live/test was unavailable during this verification window: direct container OpenCV check returned opened False/read False, so the person-entry trigger was verified with a deterministic mocked person box instead of a live human camera frame.

Direct npm.cmd run build from inside the Chinese-character frontend path failed with a Vite/Rollup absolute-path emitted asset error; npm.cmd --prefix frontend run build from the repository root passed.

features.name: Implement simple seat identity check
features.description: For active studying seats, compare the face match of a person entering the seat polygon against the reserved seat_status.user_id and raise occupy for mismatches.
required_validation: python -m py_compile backend/app/detectors/intrusion.py backend/app/api/seat_status.py backend/tests/test_intrusion_identity.py backend/tests/test_fatigue.py
python -m pytest tests/test_intrusion.py tests/test_intrusion_identity.py tests/test_fatigue.py tests/test_alarm_center.py
docker compose logs show active reserved seats
evidence: Extended IntrusionPlugin to load active studying seat regions from seat_status joined with region type=seat.
When a person enters an active reserved seat, IntrusionPlugin crops the person box, runs FaceMatcher, and compares the result to member:<seat_status.user_id>.
If the face match equals the reserved user, no alarm is produced.
If the face match differs or falls back to stranger, IntrusionPlugin raises AlarmEvent(type=occupy) with extra.kind=unauthorized_seat, expected_user_id, actual_face_match, and person_box.
POST /api/seat-status now hot-updates both fatigue and intrusion plugins, so reservation/status changes are picked up without backend restart.
Added backend/tests/test_intrusion_identity.py covering reserved-user no-alarm and unreserved-user occupy alarm.
Focused tests passed: python -m pytest tests/test_intrusion.py tests/test_intrusion_identity.py tests/test_fatigue.py tests/test_alarm_center.py -> 20 passed.
Backend container logs showed [intrusion] active reserved seats: [5], confirming runtime loaded active studying seats.

After placing dlib_face_recognition_resnet_model_v1.dat, Docker runtime verified /app/model_weights/dlib_face_recognition_resnet_model_v1.dat exists with size 22466066 and FaceMatcher.dlib_loaded=True.
Backend restart logs showed [face] FaceMatcher initialized and [face] FaceDetector ready.
